title: Staggered Downpayment Scheme
text:
Staggered Downpayment Scheme - SupportGoWhere
Staggered Downpayment Scheme
Share link
Eases home purchase by allowing eligible first-timer couples and right-sizing flat owners to pay downpayment in 2 parts.
SCHEME HIGHLIGHTS
Ease home purchase by splitting flat downpayment into two parts
Can pay in cash and / or CPF Ordinary Account (OA) savings
Collapse all sections
About this support

Downpayment split into 2 instalments for easier financial management

First instalment is paid at the signing of the Agreement for Lease and is either 5% or 10% of the purchase price (depending on your choice of housing loan)

Second instalment is paid at key collection and is either 20% or 35% of the purchase price (depending on your choice of housing loan and the Loan-To-Value (LTV) limit)

Can be paid in cash and / or CPF OA savings (including CPF housing grant, if eligible), depending on loan type

For flat applications received from young couples eligible for deferred income assessment from the Jun 2024 sales exercise:

First instalment is capped at 2.5% of the purchase price

Second instalment is either 22.5% or 42.5% of the purchase price (depending on your choice of housing loan and the LTV limit)

Applicants who acquire a housing loan with a 75% or 55% LTV limit from a Financial Institution will have to pay at least 5% or 10% of the purchase price or value (whichever is lower) in cash before disbursement

Who is this for?

First-timer couples, including those with 1 second-timer applicant

Valid HDB Flat Eligibility (HFE) letter obtained on or before the younger applicant's 30th birthday

Booked an uncompleted 5-room or smaller flat in any HDB sales exercise

Flat owners right-sizing to a 3-room or smaller flat in non-mature estates who have not completed the sale of their existing flat at the time of HFE letter application and booked an uncompleted 3-room or smaller flat in a non-mature estate in any HDB sales exercise

How to apply?

Buyers will be informed during the flat booking appointment if they are eligible.

Where can I find help?

View more details

HDB Hotline: 6866-3066

Submit an enquiry

Scheme last updated 22 May 2026
SUPPORT RECOMMENDER
Answer a few questions to find schemes and services you may be eligible for.
